events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess confined space gas meter readout O2 19.0% - enter or not? FD/DOC. Need compare against OSHA 1910.146. Need determine pass/fail/needs more info. O2 19.0% is below OSHA required 19.5% for safe entry. So do not enter. Decision fail because non-compliant and dangerous. Risk high or critical? O2 deficiency 19.0% can cause impaired judgment, risk of asphyxiation; confined space. Could be high. But if just below threshold, high. Need component conditions installed-defective maybe gas meter reading indicates atmosphere defective. Also non-compliant. Work stage tested/inspected. Percent complete maybe 100 for inspection step. Value 19.0. Findings: oxygen concentration 19.0% below 19.5% minimum; entry not permitted; atmosphere not safe. Actions: do not enter, ventilate/retest, identify source of oxygen depletion, implement forced air ventilation, retest until O2 19.5-23.5%, complete permit reauthorization. Rationale: OSHA 1910.146 requires acceptable entry conditions including oxygen between 19.5% and 23.5%; 19.0% fails. References: 29 CFR 1910.146(c)(5)(ii)(B), (d)(4)(ii), maybe. Need JSON only. Ensure valid JSON.

  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1910.146 defines acceptable entry conditions for permit-required confined spaces as an oxygen atmosphere between 19.5% and 23.5%. A reading of 19.0% O2 fails this criterion, making entry unsafe and non-compliant.",
